title: Add known-commands section to LLM prompt with correct syntax and deny-list filtering
description:
The LLM system prompt needs a curated list of available Minecraft commands with correct syntax. From user testing, the agent frequently misuses basic commands:

- `/tp` — sends wrong argument order (e.g., `/tp @p Leo` instead of `/tp Leo TheMasonX23`)
- `/summon` — targets self instead of entity at queried coordinates
- `/give` — doesn't consistently use correct syntax
- `/locate` — not in the prompt at all
- `/fill` — not in the prompt at all

The fix should:
1. Add a "known commands" section to the LLM system prompt (BuildSystemPrompt / LlmChatInterpreter)
2. Include correct syntax: `/tp <target> <destination>`, `/summon <entity> <x> <y> <z>`, `/give <player> <item> [count]`, `/locate <structure>`, `/fill <x1> <y1> <z1> <x2> <y2> <z2> <block>`, `/execute as <target> at @s run <command>`, `/time set <value>`, `/weather <type>`
3. Filter the list against DeniedCommands — don't tell the LLM about commands it can't use
4. Include guidance: "/tp can use player names or coordinates; /summon targets world coordinates not relative to bot; /give works with item IDs like oak_log, stone, dirt"

Search web for Minecraft command reference if needed for accurate syntax.
